title: AWS & DevOps
description: Become a cloud professional. Our comprehensive training program covers everything from core AWS cloud infrastructure to advanced DevOps automation pipelines.

topics: Introduction to Cloud Computing & AWS Global Infrastructure
Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) - Launching & managing Linux/Windows instances
Elastic Block Storage (EBS) - Creation, attachment, snapshots, and lifecycle management
Identity and Access Management (IAM) - Users, Groups, Policies, Roles, and MFA authentication
Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) - Subnets, Route Tables, Internet Gateways, NAT Gateways, and Security Groups
Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3) - Buckets, versioning, storage classes, policies, and lifecycle rules
Amazon Relational Database Service (RDS) - Setup, multi-AZ replication, read-replicas, and backups
Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) & Auto Scaling - High availability and horizontal scaling architectures
AWS CLI - Programmatic control of cloud resources
CloudWatch - Monitoring system metrics, logging, custom dashboards, and alerting integrations
Route-53 - Domain registration, DNS records, routing policies, and health checks
CloudFormation & Introduction to Terraform - Declarative Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
CI/CD Pipelines - Jenkins server setup, pipeline-as-code, and GitHub Actions integration
Docker Containerization - Images, Dockerfiles, volumes, networks, and container orchestration basics
Kubernetes (EKS) - Clusters, Pods, Services, Deployments, and container management
Real-World Graduation Project - Deploying a multi-tier microservice architecture in high-availability VPC

title: Full Stack Web Development
description: Build powerful web applications. Includes Frontend (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React) and Backend architectures (Node.js/Python, Databases).

topics: Semantic HTML5 & Responsive CSS3 (Flexbox, Grid, Media Queries)
CSS Frameworks - Tailwind CSS configuration and rapid UI design layout
JavaScript Essentials - Variables, DOM manipulation, ES6+ features, and async programming
Asynchronous JS - Fetch API, AJAX, Promises, and integrating public REST APIs
React.js Concepts - Components, Props, State, and Virtual DOM structure
React Hooks - useState, useEffect, useContext, and custom hook development
State Management & Routing - React Router v6, Context API, and Redux Toolkit introduction
Next.js Basics - File-based routing, Server-Side Rendering (SSR), and Static Site Generation (SSG)
Node.js Runtime - Event loop, package management (NPM), and built-in modules
Express.js Framework - Route handling, middlewares, request/response models, and error handling
RESTful API Development - Creating robust endpoints and request validations
Authentication & Security - JWT token authentication, bcrypt password hashing, and CORS
NoSQL Databases - MongoDB schemas, Mongoose ODM, CRUD operations, and aggregations
Relational Databases - PostgreSQL basics, tables, joins, and SQL query optimization
Git & GitHub - Version control workflow, branch management, pull requests, and code reviews
Deployment - Hosting frontend on Vercel, server on Render/Heroku, and database on MongoDB Atlas

title: Data Science & AI
description: Dive deep into data analysis, statistical modeling, Machine Learning algorithms, and artificial intelligence integration.

topics: Python for Data Science - Advanced data structures, list comprehensions, and lambdas
Scientific Libraries - Numerical computation with NumPy & data manipulation with Pandas
Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) - Statistical visualizations using Matplotlib & Seaborn
Mathematics & Statistics - Probability distributions, linear algebra, hypothesis testing, and regression analysis
Supervised Learning - Linear/Logistic Regression, Decision Trees, Random Forests, and SVMs
Unsupervised Learning - K-Means Clustering, Hierarchical Clustering, and Principal Component Analysis (PCA)
Model Evaluation & Tuning - Hyperparameter tuning (GridSearchCV), Cross-Validation, and Confusion Matrix metrics
Feature Engineering - Encoding, scaling, handling missing values, and dimensional reduction
Deep Learning Basics - Artificial Neural Networks (ANN), activation functions, and backpropagation
Computer Vision - Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for image recognition and processing
Natural Language Processing (NLP) - Text preprocessing, Tokenization, TF-IDF, and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN)
Frameworks - Creating and training models using TensorFlow/Keras and PyTorch
Generative AI & LLMs - Integration of OpenAI API, prompt engineering, and LangChain embeddings
Business Intelligence - Creating interactive, publication-quality dashboards in Tableau and PowerBI
Model Deployment - Building and deploying an ML app using Streamlit on Heroku/AWS Cloud
Capstone Project - Building an end-to-end predictive analysis or GenAI model on real-world Kaggle datasets

title: Python Programming
description: Perfect for absolute beginners. Learn foundational coding, object-oriented principles, scripting, and automation.

topics: Introduction to Programming - Setting up Python environment, IDLE, VS Code, and script execution
Variables & Operators - Numeric, string, boolean data types, and arithmetic/logical operators
Control Structures - Conditional statements (if-elif-else) and Loops (for, while, nested loops)
Built-in Data Structures - Mastery of Lists, Tuples, Sets, and Dictionaries
User-defined Functions - Parameters, arguments (*args, **kwargs), return statements, and local/global scope
File Handling - Reading, writing, and parsing text files, CSV files, and JSON file streams
Error & Exception Handling - Try-except-finally blocks, custom exceptions, and debugging code
Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) - Classes, Objects, Constructor (__init__), Inheritance, Polymorphism, and Encapsulation
Database Integration - Database concepts, Python DB-API, and connecting with SQLite/MySQL
CRUD Operations with DB - Writing SQL queries, fetching records, committing transactions via Python scripts
Web Scraping - Extracting data from websites using Beautiful Soup 4 and Requests libraries
Regular Expressions (RegEx) - Pattern matching, text filtering, and parsing structured strings
Desktop GUI Development - Building graphical interfaces using Tkinter library
Automation - Automated email alerts, scheduling system tasks, and file backups with os/shutil modules
Popular Modules Overview - Working with Math, DateTime, Random, and external packages via Pip
Final Practical Project - Creating a multi-functional command line or GUI tool connected to a backend database

title: Cyber Security & Ethical Hacking
description: Understand digital defense. Learn network security, vulnerability assessment, penetration testing, and risk management mitigation.

topics: Network Essentials - TCP/IP model, OSI layers, DNS, DHCP, IP addressing, and subnetting
Security Concepts - CIA Triad (Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability), Cryptography basics (Symmetric/Asymmetric)
Operating Systems Security - Windows/Linux security auditing, system log analysis, and registry permissions
Kali Linux Environment - Setting up a security lab, command line masteries, and update channels
Information Gathering - Passive & active foot-printing, OSINT techniques, Shodan, and DNS harvesting
Port Scanning & Enumeration - Network scanning with Nmap, identifying services, OS fingerprinting, and firewalls
Packet Analysis - Capturing and analyzing network traffic using Wireshark, identifying plaintext credentials
Social Engineering - Phishing simulations, credential harvesting, and security awareness controls
OWASP Top 10 - Comprehensive understanding of critical web application security risks
Injection Attacks - SQL Injection, Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), and Command Injection testing
Broken Auth & Session hijacking - Session token manipulation, brute force, and rate limiting bypasses
Vulnerability Tools - Automating scans using Burp Suite community edition and OWASP ZAP
Metasploit Framework - Exploit research, payload generation, and launching local security simulations
Privilege Escalation - Techniques to escalate user rights on Windows and Linux targets
Wireless Network Security - WPA2/WPA3 auditing, credential cracking, and rogue access points
Defense & Incident Response - Firewall setups, IDS/IPS alerts, anti-malware policies, and secure system hardening

title: Software Testing (QA)
description: Master both Manual and Automation testing frameworks using industry standards like Selenium, Java, and API Testing tools.
highlights: Automation Testing with Selenium WebDriver & Java

syllabus.module: Module 1: Manual Testing & Quality Assurance Principles
topics: Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) - Waterfall, V-Model, and Agile Scrum methodologies
Software Test Life Cycle (STLC) - Test plans, test scenarios, test cases, and test metrics design
Test Case Design Techniques - Equivalence partitioning, boundary value analysis, and decision tables
Defect Management - Bug reporting, severity vs. priority, and defect lifecycle tracking in Jira

syllabus.module: Module 2: Java Foundations for Test Automation
topics: Java Basics - JVM, variables, conditional blocks, loops, and handling user inputs
OOP in Java - Mastering Classes, Objects, Methods, Encapsulation, Polymorphism, and Collections API
Exception Handling - Custom try-catch blocks and defensive scripting styles
Build Tool Integration - Setting up Maven projects, managing POM.xml, and external dependencies

syllabus.module: Module 3: Automation Testing with Selenium WebDriver
topics: WebDriver Architecture - Connecting test scripts to Chrome, Firefox, and Edge browsers
Locator Strategies - Absolute/Relative XPaths, CSS Selectors, ID, and Name web element identification
Interactive Automation - Handling alerts, dropdowns, dynamic tables, frames, and windows
Waits & Synchronization - Implementing Implicit, Explicit, and Fluent waits in Selenium automation
TestNG Framework - Annotations, assertions, parameterization, parallel executions, and custom reports
API Testing - Manual API validation with Postman & automation with REST Assured
Page Object Model (POM) - Creating highly scalable, robust, and maintainable automation frameworks
Continuous Integration (CI) - Integration of automated test suites into Jenkins pipeline runs

title: SAP ERP Core Program
description: Master enterprise resource planning. Learn SAP FICO (Finance & Controlling) and SAP MM (Materials Management) configuration, database processes, and real-world workflows.

topics: Introduction to ERP systems & SAP GUI Navigation basics
Understanding Org Structures: Client, Company Code, Plant, and Storage Locations
Master Data Concepts: Material Master, Vendor Master, General Ledger Accounts
Transaction mapping workflows, document concepts, and SAP core T-Codes
Financial Global Settings: Fiscal Year Variant, Posting Periods, Chart of Accounts
General Ledger Configuration: Accounts creation, document posting, and journals
Accounts Payable (AP) and Accounts Receivable (AR) integration setups
Integration with Materials Management: Automatic Account Determination (OBYC)
Procurement Process setup: Requisitions (PR), Quotations, Purchase Orders (PO)
Inventory Control: Goods Receipt (GR), Goods Issue, Physical stock transfers
Logistics Invoice Verification (LIV) and credit memo configurations
Special procurement cycles: Subcontracting, Consignments, and Pipeline flows

syllabus.module: Module 4: Live Enterprise Project & Interview Prep
topics: Live Enterprise Simulation: Designing and configuring a company in SAP from scratch
System integration testing: End-to-end P2P Cycle with automatic ledger mapping
Resume design boot camp: Structuring experience for SAP Consultant and Analyst positions
Technical mock interviews, SAP Certification guidelines, and job reference listings
